output.input: {"query":"(Docker OR Docker microVM OR stateful sandbox OR vmux OR \"cloud sandbox\") lang:en","limit":10,"mode":"Latest"}

content.text:
Recent discussions on X (latest posts in English) highlight **Docker Sandbox**, a new feature introducing **microVM isolation** for safely running multiple AI coding agents (like Claude) in parallel. This enhances developer workflows by providing controlled, unsupervised environments for testing AI models.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2017456334342623403)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018504991304987131)

Key points from posts:
- Docker's blog details how sandboxes use microVMs per sandbox for better isolation, potentially improving on shared setups like WSL on Windows.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018504991304987131)
- Early reactions note it resembles advanced containerization but with VM-level security for AI tasks.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018484098092941381)

No recent mentions of "vmux", "stateful sandbox", or "cloud sandbox" in these results, though Docker Sandbox aligns closely with the themes. For the full Docker announcement: [docker.com/blog/docker-sandboxes-run-claude-code-and-other-coding-agents-unsupervised-but-safely](https://www.docker.com/blog/docker-sandboxes-run-claude-code-and-other-coding-agents-unsupervised-but-safely/).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2017456334342623403)
